attacking_style: Launches early sprint attacks from 200m out, positioned on the left side. Capitalizes on tactical misjudgments when favorites overmark pure sprinters.
team_tactics: Sunweb commits to extended tempo control from distance (90km out), setting up sprint finish rather than preserving domestiques for late positioning.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint finishes where tactical positioning and timing trump pure power; benefits when field overestimates pure sprinter threat.
vulnerability: Single race sample insufficient to identify clear weaknesses. Youth (22) suggests potential vulnerability in multi-week stage race recovery.
key_patterns: Early sprint launch at 200m rather than waiting for traditional final 150m kick.
Exploits tactical confusion when field focuses on marquee sprinter names like Kristoff.
Shows strong recovery capacity immediately post-Grand Tour with superior sprint legs.
form_trajectory: positive: demonstrated exceptional post-Giro form with sprint victory, suggesting strong recovery and peak timing
